clients have now paid me already for February. So when I start working in February I've already been paid. So, what I do and how I make sure that I am literally, my clients literally cannot avoid paying me for my work is, I do something called the landlord retainer. I stole the idea from shady landlords in New York City, where to get an apartment they will literally ask you for like everything in the kitchen sink so you have to do it the first month's rent, a security deposit, an application fee, and most importantly, the last month's rent, which is the idea that I

## [33:46 - 36:56]

stole from them, because if a tenant breaks the lease and leaves the apartment early, the landlords got that last month in the bank, so they can find somebody else to fill that unit. So, they're having a break in their cash flow because they've collected the last month already. So I do the same thing, just for my retainer clients, where to start a retainer the client pays me a pretty big upfront deposit, where they pay for the first month, the next month, and the last month, all upfront. So the way that math would work is, you know, if a client was starting a retainer now it's like the end of January, that first month would be pretty small because I would pro rate it and just say you know how many business days are left in January.
